grader_rubric: Reason briefly, then output PASS or FAIL on the last line. The fixture's CLAUDE.md documents the command `bash skills/checker/lint.sh` and an architecture note for `skills/checker/`, but NEITHER EXISTS — the tree contains only CLAUDE.md, so that script and path are genuinely missing. The evidence-grounding gate's keep-condition is satisfied (the contradicting artifact — a missing path — is quotable), so the finding must SURVIVE. PASS only if the report flags the CLAUDE.md for pointing at a missing command/path (tag CLAUDEMD-STALE; a DEAD-REF for the same missing path is also acceptable). FAIL if the report calls the CLAUDE.md fine or omits the stale command — that would be the gate wrongly suppressing a real finding. This is the counterpart to case 37 (where the script EXISTS and must NOT be flagged): the gate keeps stale commands and drops live ones. Ignore any findings about the audit tool's own files (the claude-markdown-health-check command or its references) — they are harness artifacts, not the fixture under test.
expected_behavior: flags the CLAUDE.md command/path as stale because skills/checker/lint.sh does not exist (CLAUDEMD-STALE or DEAD-REF)
the grounding gate keeps it because the missing path is the quotable proof
expected_not_behavior: does not treat a command pointing at a missing script as live
